purpose: Root Bubble Tea model that owns all sub-views and coordinates storage, refresh, and summarize operations

entry_points: IsEmpty, Match, MatchingChannelIDs, MatchingSourceNames, FilterLabel are all pure — no side effects; nil return from Matching* means 'all' (callers must check for nil vs empty slice)

cross_scope_touchpoints.surface: reads channels and message summaries, creates and deletes channels via store interface
opens real storage.Store via storage.Open; creates Channel, Message, MessageSummary records

invariants: When both Types and Names are set, Match requires both to pass (AND semantics). This is documented in the field comment but callers must be aware — the filter_overlay only ever produces Names-only filters, never Types+Names combinations.
risks: dashboard.filter is read in refreshFilteredCmd/summarizeFilteredCmd but channels.channels is also read at command-execution time — both are stale snapshots captured when the key is pressed, not at command execution. Filter may drift if channels were loading concurrently.
addURLInput is required non-empty to advance (line 159), but addNameInput trimming is done via != empty without trimming — whitespace-only names are accepted as valid.

expanded map is keyed by filtered-slice index. When a filter is applied, an index that was expanded under the previous filter now refers to a different source — stale expansion state can cause incorrect card rendering without a crash.
boxStyle is constructed on every View() call rather than cached — allocates a new lipgloss.Style on each render. Minor performance concern for high-frequency redraws.
patterns: Functional options (AppOption) pattern used for optional RefreshService and SummarizeService, keeping the constructor minimal for tests without those capabilities.
setupTestStore creates a real SQLite database in t.TempDir() — tests exercise storage integration, not mocks. mockRefreshSvc and mockSummarizeSvc are the only mocks.

Input handling in addStepName and addStepURL is manual (switch on msg.Type for KeyBackspace/KeyEnter/KeyRunes) rather than using a bubbles/textinput component — trades simplicity for lack of cursor positioning in the text field.
applyScroll is a fallback scroll helper used only for the empty-state path; the main card-rendering path uses inline scroll computation with cardStartLines tracking.
TextWrapping tests use lipgloss.Width() for ANSI-aware line width measurement — correctly accounts for escape sequences in rendered output rather than naive len().
Key matching uses direct msg.String() comparisons for space, 'a', 'n', 'enter', 'esc' rather than the global keys singleton — these are overlay-specific bindings not represented in keyMap.

Help content is hardcoded inline in View() using sections struct literal — it pulls key/desc strings from the global keys singleton via Help() method, so it stays in sync with actual bindings automatically.
Single global 'keys' var used by app.go, dashboard.go, channels.go, filter_overlay.go, and help.go — all key matching goes through this singleton rather than per-model configuration.
All visual styling is centralized here; individual model files contain zero style definitions except filter_overlay.go which constructs a local boxStyle at render time (to apply dynamic width).

Service interfaces (RefreshService, SummarizeService) are defined here in the consumer package (tui), not in the provider packages — classic dependency-inversion. This decouples the TUI from concrete refresh and summarizer implementations.
